div.p: William by the grace of God king of the Scots to the Bishops, Abbots, Earls, Barons, Iustices, Sheriffs, Provosts, Officials, and all the worthy men of the whole of his land sends greeting. Let present and future know me to have given and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the Church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place the land they hold in the territory of Clifton by the same boundaries contained in Walter of Windsor's charter which they have from him about the same land; so that they should have and possess for ever whatever is within the same boundaries just as freely and quitely, honourably and peaceably as they possess any alms in my kingdom freely, quitely and honourably. I have indeed given and confirmed it to them free from all aids, pleas, gelds, scutages, cornages, and from every secular service, custom, and exaction, so that I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant it to them against all men for ever; except for my service from Walter and his heirs. As witness: David, my brother; Earl Duncan; Earl Gilbert; William of Lindsay; Roger and Philip de Valognes; Walter of Berkeley; John of London. AT PERTH.
